page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.s2 server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores, resulting in a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external block storage volumes. Additionally, the ecs.s2.large does not feature any GPU hardware accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. These hardware constraints limit its utility for data-intensive storage operations or graphics-accelerated workloads.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents a compute-oriented resource density profile. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core environments. This makes the instance suitable for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, microservices, and development or testing environments that require stable CPU performance but have modest memory and storage footprints.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s2.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.s2 server family. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it provides 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring consistent CPU performance without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, requiring external block storage for data persistence. This resource profile, characterized by a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation with minimal memory requirements. It is suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and development environments.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with a compute-leaning resource ratio, designed for lightweight utility workloads and microservices without local storage.

4.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
